title: Constants, Literals, Domain Types, and Configuration · Gate Lens: Literal Types
summary: Replace ambiguous magic values without creating meaningless constant noise. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates literal types increment in a domain constant and configuration policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use enums, unions, literal types, branded values, or value objects for closed domains.
Name stable domain values by meaning and keep them near their owning policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
